problem: score_area_for_finding (src/output/json/helpers.rs:209) buckets a finding into a report area (Forms, Images / alternative text, etc.) via naive substring matching over rule_id + subcategory + title + description, so an unrelated finding whose free-text description happens to contain a matching substring (e.g. "conformance" contains "form"; a CSS selector like "div.alt-service-hero-card" contains "alt") gets silently misrouted into the wrong area with a fabricated "main driver".
evidence: Found by the report-critic skill's dry-run eval against reports/casoon-batch-en301549.json: summary.accessibility_score_breakdown[] listed area="Forms" with main_driver="Insufficient color contrast (27 occurrences)" (routed via the substring "form" inside the contrast finding's APCA-conformance description) and area="Images / alternative text" with main_driver="Content clipped when text spacing is increased (1 occurrences)" (routed via "alt" inside a CSS selector name) — neither area has any real forms- or image-related violation anywhere in top_recurring_rules.
expected: Area classification for a finding should key off its structured rule_id/WCAG success-criterion mapping, not substring search over free-text description/selector strings that can contain incidental matches.
counter_examples: A finding whose rule_id is genuinely forms-related (e.g. a label-association WCAG rule) must still classify into "Forms" once the fix lands — this is about false positives from incidental substrings, not about removing forms-classification entirely.
